summary: Scorching Scarlet>Fire, soaring Bird Song and tight second‑set jam
blurb: The show erupts with a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain pair that launches a high‑energy second set. Jerry’s guitar and vocals soar on Bird Song while Brent’s organ trades light up the night. Overall the performance is consistently strong, making it a must‑listen for late‑’80s Dead fans.
review: August 6, 1989 at Cal Expo captured one of the Grateful Dead’s late‑‘80s high points. The first set builds from a rollicking Let The Good Times Roll into a soaring Bird Song that many reviewers call “ferocious” and “exquisite,” with Jerry Garcia delivering a rare Stratocaster 360° solo and Brent Mydland trading organ fireworks. Set 2 detonates with the classic Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain segue, followed by a thunderous stretch of Samson & Delilah, The Other One, Wharf Rat and a fiery Turn On Your Lovelight that keeps the momentum rolling into an electrifying encore of Johnny B. Goode → And We Bid You Good Night. Across all recordings the band is praised as “locked in,” with Jerry’s vocals on fire (despite occasional hoarseness), Phil laying down a funky, musical bass line, Bob providing solid rhythm and vocal support, and Brent’s keyboard work highlighted as a night‑long standout. The soundboard sources are generally good to excellent, with the straight SBD (gd89-08-06.sbd.12831.sbeok.shnf) offering the clearest mix of these classic moments.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across all ten recordings: repeated praise for the Scarlet>Fire sequence, soaring Bird Song jam, Jerry’s ferocious guitar and vocals, Brent’s superb organ work, and overall tight band chemistry. No consistent negative comments were found.

best_recording.reason: Straight soundboard capture with crisp mix and balanced levels; clearly presents the band’s dynamics on the standout songs while avoiding the drop‑outs seen in other SBDs.

key_highlights: Smoking Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain launch that ignites Set 2
Soaring Bird Song jam with a rare Jerry Strat solo and Brent organ trade
Powerful second‑set climax featuring The Other One, Wharf Rat and Turn On Your Lovelight

band_performance.Jerry: Ferocious and exquisite on Bird Song, Scarlet>Fire and the rare Strat solo; vocals soar despite occasional hoarseness.
band_performance.Phil: Solid, musical bass throughout; described as funky and reliable.
band_performance.Bob: Provides steady rhythm guitar and vocal support; “minimally Bobish but always a treat.”
band_performance.Brent: Outstanding organ solos and trades with Jerry, especially on Bird Song; overall keyboard presence is a highlight of the night.

must_listen_sequences: And We Bid You Good Night
